2603.24125.title: Alignment Reduces Expressed but Not Encoded Gender Bias: A Unified Framework and Study
2603.24125.paper_summary: During training, Large Language Models (LLMs) learn social regularities that can lead to gender bias in downstream applications. Most mitigation efforts focus on reducing bias in generated outputs, typically evaluated on structured benchmarks, which raises two concerns: output-level evaluation does not reveal whether alignment modifies the model's underlying representations, and structured benchmarks may not reflect realistic usage scenarios. We propose a unified framework to jointly analyze intrinsic and extrinsic gender bias in LLMs using identical neutral prompts, enabling direct comparison between gender-related information encoded in internal representations and bias expressed in generated outputs. Contrary to prior work reporting weak or inconsistent correlations, we find a consistent association between latent gender information and expressed bias when measured under the unified protocol. We further examine the effect of alignment through supervised fine-tuning aimed at reducing gender bias. Our results suggest that while the latter indeed reduces expressed bias, measurable gender-related associations are still present in internal representations, and can be reactivated under adversarial prompting. Finally, we consider two realistic settings and show that debiasing effects observed on structured benchmarks do not necessarily generalize, e.g., to the case of story generation.
2603.24125.paper_summary_zh: 在訓練過程中，大型語言模型（LLMs）學習到的社會規律可能會導致下游應用中的性別偏見。大多數減少偏見的努力集中在降低生成輸出中的偏見，通常在結構化基準上進行評估，這引發了兩個擔憂：輸出級別的評估並未揭示對齊是否修改了模型的基本表示，而結構化基準可能無法反映現實使用場景。我們提出了一個統一框架，使用相同的中性提示共同分析LLMs中的內在和外在性別偏見，從而使內部表示中編碼的性別相關信息與生成輸出中表達的偏見之間能夠直接比較。與之前報告的弱或不一致的相關性相反，我們發現在統一協議下測量的潛在性別信息與表達的偏見之間存在一致的關聯。我們進一步檢查了通過有監督的微調來減少性別偏見的對齊效果。我們的結果表明，雖然後者確實減少了表達的偏見，但在內部表示中仍然存在可測量的性別相關聯繫，並且可以在對抗性提示下重新激活。最後，我們考慮了兩個現實場景，並顯示在結構化基準上觀察到的去偏見效果不一定能夠推廣，例如，對於故事生成的情況。
2603.24125.author: Nour Bouchouchi et.al.
2603.24125.authors: Nour Bouchouchi, Thiabult Laugel, Xavier Renard, Christophe Marsala, Marie-Jeanne Lesot, Marcin Detyniecki
